name: Cohort & Population
purpose: Describe who is in the study and how the cohort was defined/ascertained, enough for a user to judge relevance before requesting access.
effort: Medium (may require harmonizing terminology; some attributes may be sensitive or restricted → may keep optional where needed)

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value applies to an individual participant entity (participant-level).
The metadata value applies to a cohort or group of participants considered as a unit.

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

valid_values.definition: The metadata value represents an individual-level value (not aggregated).
The metadata value represents an aggregate summary across a defined population scope.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value summarizes or pertains to the full study population.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a specific study arm or group.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a defined cohort within the study.

fields.name: externalParticipantID
fields.description: Other external identifier, if no GUID exists

fields.name: GUID
fields.description: Global Unique Identifier: unique across studies and repositories. If a participant has been assigned a global identifier (e.g., NDAR GUID), include it here to support linkage across studies and repositories. Otherwise, leave this field blank.

fields.name: diseaseFocus
fields.description: Primary disease or condition focus of the study. A categorical descriptor summarizing participant health status selection and ascertainment context for a study cohort, intended to support discovery and comparison across studies.

valid_values.definition: Participants are selected to represent a general or normative population without requiring a specific disease or elevated risk status.
Participants are selected based on elevated risk for a condition (e.g., family history, exposure, prodromal symptoms) but may not meet diagnostic criteria.
Participants are selected because they have a specified disease or condition (or meet defined clinical criteria for that condition).
The study includes two or more contrasting groups (e.g., cases vs controls, condition vs normative, multiple conditions) to enable comparison.
Participants are identified through registries or administrative/health record sources where cohort definition is driven by existing records rather than direct recruitment.

fields.name: primaryHealthFocus
fields.description: Why participants were included: these categories describe participant health status at enrollment, not outcomes.

valid_values.definition: Participants were enrolled without requiring a specific diagnosis or elevated risk status at baseline.
Participants were enrolled because they met criteria for a specified disease/condition at baseline.
Participants were enrolled based on elevated risk indicators (e.g., family history, exposure, symptom threshold) without necessarily meeting diagnostic criteria.
The study enrolled two or more baseline health groups (e.g., condition and normative, or multiple conditions) for comparison.

fields.name: ascertainmentContext
fields.description: Where participants came from: the categories describe sampling context, independent of disease focus

valid_values.definition: Participants were recruited or identified from community sources outside of clinical care settings (e.g., advertisements, schools, workplaces).
Participants were recruited or identified through clinical care settings (e.g., clinics, hospitals, specialty practices).
Participants were identified using existing administrative sources or registries (e.g., EHR systems, claims data, disease registries).
Participants were recruited or identified using a combination of community, clinic, and/or administrative/registry sources.

fields.name: selectionIntensity
fields.description: How restrictive enrollment was: the categories describe selection methodology

valid_values.definition: Enrollment used minimal inclusion/exclusion criteria beyond basic eligibility (e.g., age range, consent capacity).
Enrollment applied multiple inclusion/exclusion criteria that narrow eligibility but remain relatively general.
Eligibility criteria are stringent or specialized, substantially narrowing eligible participants.

fields.name: populationType
fields.description: Broad classification of the population sampled in the study/cohort.

valid_values.definition: Participants are sampled to broadly represent a general population without specific health-status eligibility.
Participants are enrolled as generally healthy volunteers without a targeted disease or condition requirement.
Participants are enrolled as patients receiving care, typically in a clinical context.
Participants are enrolled based on clinical criteria, diagnosis, or engagement with clinical services.
Participants are enrolled based on indicators of elevated risk for a condition (e.g., family history, exposure, symptoms) without requiring diagnosis.
Participants are enrolled using criteria intended to increase the prevalence of a trait/condition of interest (e.g., high-risk or phenotype-enriched sampling).
Participants are enrolled because they provide care to an individual with a condition or functional need.
Participants are enrolled because they are family members of an index participant (e.g., relatives in family-based studies).
Participants are enrolled based on occupation or workplace affiliation.
Participants are enrolled from a defined workforce (e.g., employees in an organization or industry).
Participants are enrolled based on active-duty military status or affiliation.
Participants are enrolled based on veteran status.
Participants are enrolled through a school-based setting (e.g., K–12 schools) or education system.
Participants are enrolled based on student status (e.g., college or university samples).
Participants are recruited from community settings (outside clinics), such as advertisements, community orgs, or events.
Population classification is not captured by the listed categories.
Population classification is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: populationDescriptor
fields.description: Free-text descriptor capturing key population characteristics not covered by controlled terms (e.g., “male veterans”, “older adults with caregiver support”).

fields.name: ageGroup
fields.description: Broad age-based population grouping represented in the cohort.

valid_values.definition: Cohort includes participants primarily aged 65 years and older.
Cohort includes participants spanning multiple age groups.
Age group composition is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: sex
fields.description: The biological classification of a participant, typically assigned at birth based on physical and genetic characteristics.

valid_values.definition: Sex recorded as female.
Sex recorded as male.
Sex recorded as intersex or having variations of sex characteristics.
Sex is not known or not recorded.
Sex was not provided by the participant or was intentionally not reported.

fields.name: sexDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by sex category.

fields.name: sexEligible
fields.description: Sex eligibility criteria for cohort inclusion (if the study restricts enrollment).

valid_values.definition: Only participants recorded as female are eligible for enrollment.
Only participants recorded as male are eligible for enrollment.
Participants of any sex are eligible for enrollment.
Sex eligibility criteria are not specified in available documentation.
Sex eligibility criteria are not known or not recorded.

fields.name: gender
fields.description: The socially and culturally constructed identity that describes a person’s self-identified gender, which may or may not correspond to sex assigned at birth and may change over time.

valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as a woman.
Participant self-identifies as a man.
Participant self-identifies as non-binary.
Participant self-identifies with a gender identity not listed (e.g., genderqueer, agender); details may be captured elsewhere if allowed.
Participant chose not to disclose their gender identity.
Gender identity is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: genderDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified gender.
fields.example: Woman: 285 (57%); Man: 207 (41.4%); Nonbinary/another identity: 5 (1%); Prefer not to answer: 3 (0.6%)

fields.name: race
fields.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A socially defined classification that reflects a participant’s self-identified racial group(s), based on shared social, historical, or cultural experiences, and not biological or genetic differences.

valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as American Indian or Alaska Native.
Participant self-identifies as Asian.
Participant self-identifies as Black or African American.

valid_values.value: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander
valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander.
Participant self-identifies as White.
Participant self-identifies as a race not listed.
Participant chose not to disclose their race.
Race is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: raceDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified race category or categories.
fields.example: White: 300 (60%); Black/African American: 80 (16%); Asian: 55 (11%); American Indian/Alaska Native: 10 (2%); Multiracial/Other: 45 (9%); Unknown: 10 (2%)

fields.name: ethnicity
fields.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A self-identified cultural classification that reflects whether a participant identifies as being of Hispanic or Latino origin, independent of race.

valid_values.definition: Participant self-identifies as Hispanic or Latino origin.
Participant self-identifies as not Hispanic or Latino origin.
Participant chose not to disclose ethnicity.

fields.name: ethnicityDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified ethnicity.

fields.name: generation
fields.description: Generation within family cohort (ex., F0=original, F1=child, F2=grandchild). Indicates the generational position of participants within a family-based cohort, relative to the originally enrolled participant(s).

valid_values.definition: Original enrolled participant(s) forming the initial cohort generation.
First generation descendants of the original participant(s).
Second generation descendants of the original participant(s).
Third or later generation descendants of the original participant(s).
Participants span multiple generational levels.
Generational position is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: relationToOriginal
fields.description: Relationship to original participant (ex., self, child, grandchild, spouse). Describes the familial relationship(s) of participants to the initially enrolled participant(s) in a family-based cohort.

valid_values.definition: The participant is the original enrolled participant (index participant).
The participant is a biological or adopted child of the original participant.
The participant is a biological or adopted grandchild of the original participant.
The participant is a biological or adoptive parent of the original participant.
The participant is a biological or adoptive sibling of the original participant.
The participant is a spouse or long-term partner of the original participant.
The participant is a family relation not otherwise specified.
Multiple relationship types are represented but not specified individually.
Relationship to the original participant is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: constructDomain
fields.description: Domain of measured construct

valid_values.definition: Constructs related to depressive symptoms, mood disturbance, or depression-related functioning.
Constructs related to cognitive processes (e.g., attention, memory, executive function).
Constructs related to neurological function and neurobehavioral symptoms (e.g., motor, sleep, pain).
Constructs related to physical health status and health-related behaviors (e.g., activity, diet, cardiometabolic health).
Constructs related to development, adaptive functioning, and daily living skills.
Constructs related to environmental exposures and contextual factors (e.g., SES, life events, seasonality).
Constructs related to administrative descriptors or study design variables (e.g., demographics, medication use).
Construct domain not captured by the listed categories.
Construct domain is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: eligibilityCriteriaSummary
fields.description: A brief summary of the major inclusion and exclusion criteria that determined who could enroll in the study/cohort.
fields.example: Participants ages 18–65; able to provide consent; English-speaking; no major neurological disorder or contraindication to study procedures.

fields.name: eligibilityCriteriaURL
fields.description: Link to the protocol, registry record, or document containing full inclusion/exclusion criteria.

fields.name: inclusionTags
fields.description: High-level inclusion characteristics used to define the enrolled population.

valid_values.definition: Inclusion required meeting specified age criteria (e.g., age range or minimum/maximum age).
Inclusion required meeting specified sex eligibility criteria (e.g., female-only or male-only enrollment).
Inclusion required a documented diagnosis or meeting diagnostic criteria for a condition.
Inclusion required meeting a specified symptom level or screening threshold.
Inclusion required elevated risk indicators such as family history, genetic risk, or relevant exposure.
Inclusion required a specified treatment status or medication use/non-use.
Inclusion required ability to communicate in a specified language or meet communication criteria.
Inclusion required residing in or being served by a defined geographic area or recruitment catchment.
Inclusion required access to or use of a device (e.g., smartphone) needed for participation.
Inclusion required capacity to consent or meeting consent/assent requirements.
Inclusion characteristics not captured by the listed categories.
Inclusion characteristics are not known or not recorded.

fields.name: exclusionTags
fields.description: High-level exclusion characteristics used to restrict enrollment.
fields.example: Inability to consent excluded, Neurological comorbidity excluded

valid_values.definition: Exclusion criteria removed participants with significant comorbid medical conditions.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with specified psychiatric comorbidities.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with specified neurological comorbidities.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with current or past substance use conditions as defined by the study.
Exclusion criteria removed participants with cognitive impairment beyond a specified threshold.
Exclusion criteria removed participants who were pregnant (as defined by the study protocol).
Exclusion criteria removed participants with sensory or motor limitations that would interfere with participation or measurement.
Exclusion criteria removed participants using medications that were contraindicated or would confound measurement.
Exclusion criteria removed participants unable to provide informed consent (or meet assent/consent requirements).
Exclusion criteria removed participants at elevated risk of noncompliance (e.g., inability to follow procedures or complete assessments).
Exclusion characteristics not captured by the listed categories.
Exclusion characteristics are not known or not recorded.

fields.name: criteriaSelectionIntensity
fields.description: Overall restrictiveness of inclusion/exclusion criteria.
